summary: A brief encyclopedia entry summarizing Martin Heidegger's role in 20th-century philosophy. It highlights his magnum opus, Being and Time, his critique of the Cartesian subject, and his introduction of the concept of Dasein.

principles.statement: Human existence is fundamentally understood as Dasein ('being-there'), which is groundless and engaged in a struggle for authenticity.
principles.rationale: Dasein is inexorably projected towards the nothingness of its being, invalidating the traditional pretensions of the Cartesian subject.

mental_models.name: Dasein ("being-there")
mental_models.description: A model of human existence that replaces the Cartesian subject, viewing human life as groundless, projected towards nothingness, and defined by its struggle for authenticity.

anti_patterns.description: Accepting the claims and pretensions of the Cartesian subject.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to recognize the groundless nature of Dasein and its inexorable projection towards nothingness.
